explanation: The natural language requirement states that after the start-up process has finished successfully (represented by 'startUpDone'), the system cannot transition to self-test mode ('SelfTestMode') unless the user has first selected either 'New Patient' ('newPatient') or 'Resume Ventilation' ('resumeVentilation'). In LTL, this is typically represented by saying it is globally always true that when 'startUpDone' holds, then before 'SelfTestMode' becomes true, at some point in between, either 'newPatient' or 'resumeVentilation' must have occurred. This is accurately modeled by: G((startUpDone & F SelfTestMode) -> (F (newPatient | resumeVentilation) U SelfTestMode)). This means: globally, whenever startUpDone occurs and eventually SelfTestMode will occur, then it must be the case that either newPatient or resumeVentilation happens before SelfTestMode. Alternative formulas that simply use implication or do not enforce the order (that the user selection occurs between startUpDone and SelfTestMode) would not capture the required constraint. This LTL property specifically enforces the temporal dependency required by the original statement.
output_LTL: G((startUpDone & F SelfTestMode) -> (F (newPatient | resumeVentilation) U SelfTestMode))

explanation: The requirement states that, after successful completion of start-up (startUpDone), before the system can enter self-test mode (SelfTestMode), the user must select either New Patient (newPatient) or Resume Ventilation (resumeVentilation). In LTL, we can capture this by specifying that globally, whenever SelfTestMode becomes true, it must be preceded at some point after startUpDone by either newPatient or resumeVentilation, and without another occurrence of SelfTestMode in between. The standard LTL way to capture 'must occur before' semantics for a particular transition is to require that whenever SelfTestMode holds, there was a previous newPatient or resumeVentilation since startUpDone. This is typically captured using the 'release' and 'until' operators, but a more direct way is: globally, if startUpDone has occurred and until newPatient or resumeVentilation, SelfTestMode does not occur. This formula prevents SelfTestMode from occurring after startUpDone until either newPatient or resumeVentilation happen. This behavior correctly enforces the dependency, assuming startUpDone, newPatient, resumeVentilation, and SelfTestMode are atomic propositions as described. This property avoids some common mistakes, such as only requiring the selection once (ignoring multiple transitions), or not handling repeated entries into SelfTestMode.
output_LTL: G ( ( startUpDone & ! ( newPatient | resumeVentilation ) ) -> !SelfTestMode U ( newPatient | resumeVentilation ) )

explanation: The natural language states that after start-up is completed, the user must select either “New Patient” or “Resume Ventilation” before the system can enter self-test mode. In LTL, this dependency is expressed in two parts: (1) whenever startUpDone holds (start-up completing), it must be followed (before any SelfTestMode) by either newPatient or resumeVentilation, and (2) the system should not enter SelfTestMode before newPatient or resumeVentilation is true after startUpDone. The formula 'G((startUpDone & X(F(SelfTestMode))) -> F((newPatient | resumeVentilation) & X SelfTestMode))' ensures that whenever startUpDone happens and SelfTestMode will eventually occur, then eventually newPatient or resumeVentilation happens and SelfTestMode follows in the next step. This captures the requirement that SelfTestMode is only entered after a user selection post-start-up. The previously possible encoding like 'G(startUpDone -> F(newPatient | resumeVentilation))' would not sufficiently tie the user selection specifically to the transition before SelfTestMode and would be too weak.
output_LTL: G((startUpDone & X(F(SelfTestMode))) -> F((newPatient | resumeVentilation) & X SelfTestMode))

explanation:
The requirement is that after the start-up has been completed successfully, the user must select either 'New Patient' or 'Resume Ventilation' before the system transitions to self-test mode. In terms of atomic propositions, 'startUpDone' signals successful start-up, 'newPatient' signals 'New Patient' is selected, 'resumeVentilation' signals 'Resume Ventilation' is selected, and 'SelfTestMode' signals the system is in self-test mode. The most accurate LTL property expressing the requirement is: Globally, it is never the case that, after startUpDone, we reach SelfTestMode before either newPatient or resumeVentilation occurs. 

This can be captured as: G((startUpDone & !(newPatient | resumeVentilation)) -> (!SelfTestMode U (newPatient | resumeVentilation)))

This formula reads: From any state in which start-up has been completed and neither 'New Patient' nor 'Resume Ventilation' has yet occurred, SelfTestMode cannot hold until one of 'New Patient' or 'Resume Ventilation' has occurred. After that, SelfTestMode may be entered. This captures the requirement by ensuring that SelfTestMode is not entered between startUpDone and the first of (newPatient | resumeVentilation). Any naive LTL formula that does not relate the ordering between 'startUpDone', 'newPatient'/'resumeVentilation', and 'SelfTestMode' would be inadequate.

The natural language states that after (once) the start-up process is completed successfully (represented by the atomic proposition 'startUpDone'), the system must not transition to SelfTest mode (represented by 'SelfTestMode') until the user has selected either 'New Patient' or 'Resume Ventilation' (captured by the disjunction 'newPatient | resumeVentilation'). The LTL formula ensures that whenever 'startUpDone' occurs, it is always the case that 'SelfTestMode' cannot become true until either 'newPatient' or 'resumeVentilation' becomes true. This is captured by the formula G(startUpDone -> (!SelfTestMode U (newPatient | resumeVentilation))), which is the standard LTL way to capture 'A cannot happen until B or C has happened, AFTER X', in this case after 'startUpDone'. This property directly and correctly encodes the ordering and enabling constraints described. No previous LTL property is given for comparison, but any that omit the sequencing or the simultaneous options for enabling SelfTestMode would be less precise.

The requirement states that after start-up is completed successfully (startUpDone), the user must select either 'New Patient' (newPatient) or 'Resume Ventilation' (resumeVentilation) before the system transitions to self-test mode (SelfTestMode). Translating this into LTL: This means that whenever SelfTestMode becomes true (i.e., upon a transition or when it starts to hold), it must have been preceded (possibly immediately before or at any point before) by startUpDone, and after that, before entering SelfTestMode, either newPatient or resumeVentilation must have been set. We can formulate this as: Globally, whenever SelfTestMode holds, it must be the case that in the past (since startUpDone), it was the case that newPatient or resumeVentilation occurred latest before SelfTestMode (with no SelfTestMode in between). In LTL (future-looking), this can be captured by: it is always the case that after startUpDone, we cannot reach SelfTestMode unless newPatient or resumeVentilation has happened since startUpDone and before SelfTestMode. The property below expresses: after startUpDone, until either newPatient or resumeVentilation, SelfTestMode must not occur; and once newPatient or resumeVentilation is chosen (once after startUpDone), at some point SelfTestMode can occur. This ensures the ordering: startUpDone -> (newPatient | resumeVentilation) -> SelfTestMode.

The requirement says that after completing start-up successfully (indicated by 'startUpDone'), the next required user action before entering self-test mode ('SelfTestMode') is selection of either 'newPatient' or 'resumeVentilation'. That is, at any time if start-up is finished and the system has not yet entered 'SelfTestMode', then before it can enter 'SelfTestMode', either 'newPatient' or 'resumeVentilation' must occur. The appropriate LTL formula uses the "until" operator to specify that from any point where 'startUpDone' is true, it is always the case that 'SelfTestMode' cannot occur before ('newPatient' or 'resumeVentilation') occurs. This is expressed as: G((startUpDone & !SelfTestMode) -> (!(SelfTestMode) U (newPatient | resumeVentilation))). This formula captures that after 'startUpDone', any path to 'SelfTestMode' must pass through '(newPatient | resumeVentilation)'. No previous formula is provided, so no comparison is needed.

The input specifies that after successful start-up (indicated by startUpDone), before the transition to self-test mode (SelfTestMode), the user must select either 'New Patient' (newPatient) or 'Resume Ventilation' (resumeVentilation). The temporal interpretation is: Whenever startUpDone happens and before SelfTestMode becomes true, newPatient or resumeVentilation must occur. In LTL, this is generally captured by stating that globally, whenever startUpDone holds, it is always the case that until SelfTestMode happens, (newPatient | resumeVentilation) must happen at least once. This is expressed as: G(startUpDone -> ((!(SelfTestMode)) U ((newPatient | resumeVentilation) & (!(SelfTestMode) U SelfTestMode)))). This formula ensures that after startUpDone, before SelfTestMode is entered, at least one of newPatient or resumeVentilation occurs. Simpler versions without the nested U may not guarantee immediate requirement just before SelfTestMode, or may allow entering SelfTestMode without enforcing the user selection.

The requirement specifies that after the successful completion of start-up (represented by startUpDone), the user must select either 'New Patient' or 'Resume Ventilation' before transitioning the system to self-test mode (SelfTestMode). This is a classic 'pre-condition' pattern—the actions newPatient or resumeVentilation must occur before SelfTestMode can become true, after startUpDone occurs. The LTL property expresses that, from the moment where startUpDone becomes true, it is always the case that if SelfTestMode becomes true, then newPatient or resumeVentilation must have occurred at some time after startUpDone and before SelfTestMode. Formally, this is captured by the pattern: whenever startUpDone happens, SelfTestMode is not allowed until (U) newPatient or resumeVentilation has happened. Thus: G(startUpDone -> (!(SelfTestMode) U (newPatient | resumeVentilation))). The formula forbids the system from entering SelfTestMode until after one of the permitted selections is made, whenever startUpDone occurs. Alternative encodings that do not use the strong until pattern (U), or that do not key off the startUpDone moment, may miss the requirement to only begin this mandate after start-up completion.

The requirement states that after the start-up has been completed successfully ('startUpDone'), the user must select either 'New Patient' ('newPatient') or 'Resume Ventilation' ('resumeVentilation') before the system transitions to self-test mode ('SelfTestMode'). This means that from the moment start-up is done until the first time the system enters self-test mode, the user must select at least one of 'New Patient' or 'Resume Ventilation'. The LTL formula: G((startUpDone & !SelfTestMode) -> (!(SelfTestMode) U (newPatient | resumeVentilation))) states that globally, if start-up has completed and the system has not yet transitioned to self-test mode, then the system cannot transition to self-test mode until at least one of (newPatient OR resumeVentilation) is true. This prevents an immediate transition to self-test mode after start-up is done without the user making the required selection.
